subject: [PATCH v6 0/6] fuse: add io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: Reviewer Miklos Szeredi questioned whether fuse_uring_register() should block if the fuse ring is not yet set up, highlighting a potential edge case.
reviews.raw_body:
What about if the fuse ring is not yet set up?  Should
fuse_uring_register() wait in that case?

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the synchronization between INIT is not sorted out and requested it be addressed before considering the patch for inclusion.
reviews.raw_body:
The interface changes make sense and the benchmark results look impressive.

I think if the synchronization between INIT is sorted out then this is
good to go.  Sashiko can't apply the series, I guess because of the
non-standard base.   Not sure if we can help that.

Also because Bernd and you are co-maintainers of fuse-uring, I'd hope
for an ack from Bernd (when he returns from vacation).

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed a concern about potential races between the kernel processing FUSE_INIT and userspace issuing REGISTER, explaining that the ring is published before fch->initialized gets set, resulting in the same -EAGAIN behavior as pre-existing code.
reviews.raw_body:
The fuse ring is set up when the kernel processes the FUSE_INIT reply.
If userspace races ahead and issues a REGISTER before the FUSE_INIT
reply is processed, they get back -EAGAIN and will need to retry,
which is the same as the pre-existing behavior. The ring gets
published before fch->initialized gets set.

Not sure if this answers your question or if I'm misreading what you're asking.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: Reviewer questioned whether the REGISTER operation should block until initialization is complete, suggesting it would be a good behavior to ensure the connection is initialized before proceeding.
reviews.raw_body:
That was my question, yes.

Retrying in a loop doesn't sound like good behavior.

I was thinking that with sync INIT the completing the write() with the
INIT reply does not mean that the connection gets initialized.

Shouldn't the REGISTER op block until initialization is complete?

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the sync case would be more ideal and agreed to address it in a separate patch, not as part of the current series.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, this would be more ideal for the sync case. libfuse currently
works around this with the retry loop.

This is a pre-existing issue that's not introduced by this series
though, so I'll send a patch out for this separately.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledges that the INIT synchronization issue is separate and will be addressed in a future patch, indicating no immediate fix for this specific concern.

Author is asking reviewer Bernd to prioritize the patch series for the upcoming 7.3 merge window, citing cross-coordination with Jens's tree.
reviews.raw_body:
I'll submit a patch for the INIT synchronization separately to avoid
respinning a v7, since it's a pre-existing issue not introduced by
this series.

---

Bernd, this is targeting the 7.3 merge window, which opens August
16th. Because it involves cross-coordination with Jens's tree, I'd
prefer to get this in at the start of the window. Can you please look
at this series and make it a priority before then?

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for io-uring buffer pools to the FUSE file system, allowing servers to pass in a contiguous chunk of memory that the kernel can use to service requests. This decouples the request and payload buffers, reducing memory usage requirements and enabling optimizations for assigning payload memory. Additionally, the series introduces zero-copy functionality, which allows servers with CAP_SYS_ADMIN privileges to directly access client pages or page cache folios without copying data through an intermediary buffer. The zero-copy feature requires using buffer pools and has a dependency on io-uring registered bvec changes. The patch series includes benchmark results showing significant throughput improvements for direct-I/O operations, particularly when backing I/O is not the bottleneck.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a potential issue with libfuse registration order, but suggested a fix in user-space instead of kernel code.
reviews.raw_body:
We can fix up libfuse [1] to no longer send REGISTER before the
connection is initialized, so I don't think a kernel fix is strictly
needed here, but if you still prefer kernel code to coordinate this, I
can send that out.

Thanks,
Joanne

[1] https://github.com/libfuse/libfuse/pull/1561

Additionally, the series introduces zero-copy functionality, which allows servers with CAP_SYS_ADMIN privileges to directly access client pages or page cache folios without copying data through an intermediary buffer. The zero-copy feature requires using buffer pools and has a dependency on io-uring registered bvec changes.
